seed_problem: Maximize total weight of satisfied clauses in a synthetic weighted CNF; checker computes satisfied weight / baseline.
mutation: goal+output: replace the disjunctive weighted Max-SAT clauses with conjunctive requirement patterns (each opportunity is served only if ALL its listed station-alignment literals match), turning the instance into a weighted MAX-CSP / max-satisfied-conjunctions problem. This makes the do-nothing (all-0) baseline genuinely weak (it serves only ~2^-k of patterns), so real search is required and heuristics diverge, while a single global flip cannot win.
title: Antenna Alignment for Satellite Ground Stations
strategies: all-0 baseline: lock every station to alignment 0 (the calibration point, ratio 0.1)
weighted pattern-greedy: process opportunities in decreasing revenue and fix the required alignments of any opportunity still fully fixable, leaving untouched stations at 0
multi-restart bit-flip local search: hill-climb single-station flips from the greedy start, the all-0 start, and several seeded random starts, keeping the best-revenue alignment
per-station weighted majority relaxation followed by conflict-aware repair of the heaviest unsatisfied patterns
